classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Petilianus said: "‘Blessed are they that mourn: for they shall be comforted.’

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: You, our butchers, are the cause of mourning in others: you do not mourn yourselves."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: Consider for a short space to how many, and with what intensity, the cry of "Praises be to God," proceeding from your armed men, has caused others to mourn.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Do you say again, What is that to us?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Then I too will rejoin again your own words, What is that to us?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: What is it to all the nations of the earth?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: What is it to those who praise the name of the Lord from the rising of the sun to the setting of the same?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: What is it to all the earth, which sings a new song?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: What is it to the seed of Abraham, in which all the nations of the earth are blessed?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: And so the sacrilege of your schism is chargeable on you, just because the evil deeds of your companions are not chargeable on you; and because you are from this that the deeds of those on whose account you separated from the world, even if you proved your charges to be true, do not involve the world in sin.
